summary: Test suite validates happy path through GitHub Actions (package.json, requirements.txt, mixed license statuses) and includes good structural checks (actionlint, shellcheck, workflow triggers). However, it is severely lacking: (1) no error handling tests despite implementation having many error paths (missing manifest, invalid JSON, malformed files, bad CLI args); (2) no unit tests for critical functions like parse_manifest(), lookup_license(), classify() - all testing through slow act Docker integration; (3) missing edge cases (empty manifests, whitespace, duplicates, version matching logic, wildcard DB lookup, deny precedence); (4) fixture files not shown, can't verify correctness; (5) assertions use fragile substring matching; (6) would not catch regressions in error paths or boundary conditions. Rebuilding with direct unit tests (bats fixtures + mocks) alongside act integration tests would dramatically improve trustworthiness.
